en_definition: a person who owns a house or apartment and rents it to other people
example1: The landlord fixed the broken window in my apartment.
example1_ru: Хозяин квартиры починил разбитое окно в моей квартире.
example2: My landlord raises the rent every year in summer.

en_definition: a person who rents a house or apartment from the owner
example1: The tenant must pay rent on the first day of each month.
example1_ru: Арендатор должен платить за аренду в первый день каждого месяца.
example2: Our new tenants are very quiet and friendly neighbors.

en_definition: a formal agreement that allows someone to rent a property for a fixed period
example1: I signed a one-year lease for my student apartment.
example1_ru: Я подписал договор аренды на год для своей студенческой квартиры.
example2: The lease says I cannot have pets in the room.

en_definition: money that you give as security when you rent something, returned if nothing is damaged
example1: I paid a security deposit of two thousand yuan when I moved in.
example1_ru: Я заплатил залог в две тысячи юаней, когда въехал.
example2: The landlord returned my deposit after I moved out.

en_definition: a kitchen appliance that keeps food and drinks cold
example1: I keep milk and butter in the fridge to keep them fresh.
example1_ru: Я храню молоко и масло в холодильнике, чтобы они оставались свежими.
example2: The fridge in our dorm is broken, so we need a new one.

en_definition: the cold part of a refrigerator for storing frozen food
example1: I store ice cream and frozen vegetables in the freezer.
example1_ru: Я храню мороженое и замороженные овощи в морозильной камере.
example2: The freezer keeps food frozen for many months.

en_definition: a kitchen device that cooks food quickly using heat waves
example1: I heat my coffee in the microwave for two minutes.
example1_ru: Я разогреваю кофе в микроволновке две минуты.
example2: The microwave makes a loud beep when the food is ready.

en_definition: a machine that cleans dishes and plates automatically
example1: I load the dirty dishes into the dishwasher every evening.
example1_ru: Каждый вечер я загружаю грязную посуду в посудомоечную машину.
example2: Our new dishwasher saves us a lot of time washing plates.

en_definition: to put furniture and other items in a room or building
example1: We need to furnish our new flat with a bed and chairs.
example1_ru: Нам нужно обставить новую квартиру кроватью и стульями.
example2: The landlord furnished the apartment with basic furniture.

en_definition: something permanently fixed to a building, like a light or a sink
example1: The light fixture on the ceiling is broken and needs repair.
example1_ru: Светильник на потолке сломан, и его нужно починить.
example2: The bathroom fixtures include a shower and bathtub.

en_definition: to repair and improve a building or room to make it look new
example1: My parents are renovating the kitchen with new cabinets.
example1_ru: Мои родители делают ремонт на кухне с новыми шкафами.
example2: They renovated the old house and made it beautiful.

en_definition: to make a room or building look nice by adding things to it
example1: I decorate my room with posters and photos of my friends.
example1_ru: Я украшаю свою комнату постерами и фотографиями друзей.
example2: She decorated the house with flowers for the party.

en_definition: fabric that hangs over a window to keep out light or give privacy
example1: I close the curtains before I go to bed at night.
example1_ru: Я задёргиваю шторы перед сном.
example2: The curtains in my bedroom are blue and very soft.

en_definition: large objects like chairs, tables, and beds that you put in a room
example1: We bought new furniture for the living room last month.
example1_ru: В прошлом месяце мы купили новую мебель для гостиной.
example2: The furniture in the dormitory is old but comfortable.

en_definition: a flat piece of wood or metal attached to a wall, for putting things on
example1: I put my books on the shelf above my desk.
example1_ru: Я ставлю свои книги на полку над столом.
example2: The kitchen shelves are full of plates and glasses.

en_definition: a soft pad that you sit or rest on for comfort
example1: I put cushions on the sofa to make it more comfortable.
example1_ru: Я кладу подушки на диван, чтобы он был удобнее.
example2: The chair has a soft cushion on the seat.

en_definition: a piece of thick woven material that covers part of a floor
example1: We have a large rug in the living room under the table.
example1_ru: У нас в гостиной под столом лежит большой ковёр.
example2: The soft rug feels nice under my bare feet.

en_definition: a member of your family, such as cousin, uncle, or aunt
example1: My relatives from the countryside visit us every summer.
example1_ru: Родственники из деревни навещают нас каждое лето.
example2: I have many relatives living in different cities.

en_definition: a brother or a sister
example1: I have two siblings: an older brother and a younger sister.
example1_ru: У меня двое братьев и сестёр: старший брат и младшая сестра.
example2: My siblings and I share a bedroom in our small apartment.

en_definition: a husband or wife
example1: My spouse and I like to cook dinner together every evening.
example1_ru: Мы с супругом любим готовить ужин вместе каждый вечер.
example2: She traveled abroad with her spouse last year.

en_definition: a baby, especially less than one year old
example1: The infant sleeps about sixteen hours every day.
example1_ru: Младенец спит около шестнадцати часов в сутки.
example2: My sister has a newborn infant who cries a lot.

en_definition: an adult who is chosen to take care of a child and be responsible if parents cannot
example1: My godparent takes me to the cinema for my birthday.
example1_ru: Мой крёстный водит меня в кино на день рождения.
example2: The godparents promised to help raise the child.

en_definition: to have a disagreement with someone, usually with raised voices
example1: My brother and I often argue about computer games.
example1_ru: Мы с братом часто ссоримся из-за компьютерных игр.
example2: They argued for an hour about whose turn it was to cook.

en_definition: an angry disagreement between people who are usually close to each other
example1: The quarrel between my parents lasted for three days.
example1_ru: Ссора между моими родителями длилась три дня.
example2: They had a quarrel over money and did not speak for a week.

en_definition: to stop being angry with someone and become friendly again
example1: My sister and I made up after arguing about homework.
example1_ru: Мы с сестрой помирились после ссоры из-за домашнего задания.
example2: They had a fight but made up at the dinner table.

en_definition: a small job or task that you have to do regularly, especially at home
example1: My chore is to wash the dishes every evening after dinner.
example1_ru: Моя обязанность по дому — мыть посуду каждый вечер после ужина.
example2: The children have different chores like sweeping and cooking.

en_definition: clothes and fabrics that need to be washed, or the act of washing them
example1: I do laundry every Saturday morning in the dormitory.
example1_ru: Я стираю бельё каждую субботу утром в общежитии.
example2: My mother does laundry twice a week for the whole family.

en_definition: to make clothes smooth by using a hot iron tool
example1: I iron my shirts on Sunday evening for the next week.
example1_ru: Я глажу рубашки в воскресенье вечером на следующую неделю.
example2: She irons all the family's clothes every Wednesday.

en_definition: to clean a floor using a machine that sucks up dirt
example1: I vacuum the living room every Friday afternoon.
example1_ru: Я пылесошу гостиную каждую пятницу после обеда.
example2: Please vacuum the bedroom before your guests arrive.

ru_translation: ездить на работу/учебу, совершать ежедневные поездки
en_definition: to travel regularly from your home to work or school
example1: I commute to campus every day by bicycle.
example1_ru: Я езжу в университет на велосипеде каждый день.
example2: Many students commute forty minutes on the subway.

en_definition: a regular and usual way of doing things or a pattern of activities
example1: My morning routine is to wake up, shower, and eat breakfast.
example1_ru: По утрам я обычно просыпаюсь, принимаю душ и завтракаю.
example2: Students need a good study routine to pass their exams.

en_definition: a plan that shows the times when something will happen
example1: My class schedule shows that I have English at nine o'clock.
example1_ru: Моё расписание занятий показывает, что английский у меня в девять часов.
example2: The train schedule says the bus arrives at five thirty.

en_definition: a device that makes a sound at a set time to wake you up
example1: I set my alarm clock for seven o'clock every school day.
example1_ru: Я ставлю будильник на семь часов каждый учебный день.
example2: My phone has an alarm clock app that wakes me up.

en_definition: to stop sleeping or to make someone stop sleeping
example1: I wake up at six o'clock every morning for class.
example1_ru: Каждое утро я просыпаюсь в шесть часов, чтобы успеть на занятия.
example2: The alarm woke me up very early today.

en_definition: to put on clothes
example1: I get dressed quickly before my first class.
example1_ru: Я быстро одеваюсь перед первым уроком.
example2: He got dressed in his favorite blue shirt for the party.

en_definition: to heat liquid to the point where it becomes very hot and bubbles form
example1: I boil water for tea every morning at breakfast.
example1_ru: Каждое утро я кипячу воду для чая на завтрак.
example2: The pasta needs to boil for ten minutes until it is soft.

en_definition: to cook food in hot oil in a pan or deep fryer
example1: I fry eggs in a little oil every morning for breakfast.
example1_ru: Каждое утро я жарю яйца на небольшом количестве масла на завтрак.
example2: She fried chicken and vegetables for dinner tonight.

ru_translation: готовить на гриле, жарить на решетке
en_definition: to cook food on a metal frame over direct heat
example1: We grill meat and vegetables in summer on our balcony.
example1_ru: Летом мы жарим мясо и овощи на гриле на балконе.
example2: The chef grilled fish with lemon and garlic sauce.

en_definition: to cook meat or vegetables in an oven using dry heat
example1: My mother roasts chicken with potatoes for Sunday dinner.
example1_ru: Моя мама запекает курицу с картошкой на воскресный обед.
example2: We roasted vegetables in the oven for an hour.

en_definition: to cook something like bread or cake in an oven
example1: I bake bread and cookies for my family every weekend.
example1_ru: Я пеку хлеб и печенье для своей семьи каждые выходные.
example2: She bakes a chocolate cake for celebrations.

en_definition: to rub food against a rough surface to make small pieces
example1: I grate cheese and carrots for the salad every day.
example1_ru: Каждый день я тру сыр и морковь для салата.
example2: She grated chocolate to put on top of the dessert.

en_definition: to cut something into small pieces with a knife
example1: I chop onions and garlic for cooking almost every day.
example1_ru: Я почти каждый день режу лук и чеснок для готовки.
example2: Please chop the vegetables into small pieces for the soup.

en_definition: to mix something with a spoon or other tool
example1: I stir the soup until all the ingredients are mixed together.
example1_ru: Я помешиваю суп, пока все ингредиенты не смешаются.
example2: Stir the coffee well before you add milk and sugar.

ru_translation: томить, варить на медленном огне, кипеть на слабом огне
en_definition: to cook liquid or food at a temperature just below boiling point
example1: Let the sauce simmer for twenty minutes on low heat.
example1_ru: Дайте соусу потомиться двадцать минут на слабом огне.
example2: The stew needs to simmer for two hours until tender.

en_definition: to mix different things together completely
example1: I blend fruit and yogurt to make a healthy smoothie.
example1_ru: Я смешиваю в блендере фрукты и йогурт, чтобы сделать полезный смузи.
example2: Blend the ingredients until the mixture is completely smooth.

ru_translation: приправлять, солить и перчить, добавлять специи
en_definition: to add salt, pepper, or spices to food to make it taste better
example1: Season the soup with salt and pepper before serving.
example1_ru: Приправьте суп солью и перцем перед подачей на стол.
example2: I season my rice with garlic and olive oil.

ru_translation: украсить блюдо зеленью, украшение на тарелке
en_definition: to add a small decoration to food to make it look nice
example1: Garnish the plate with fresh parsley and lemon slices.
example1_ru: Украсьте блюдо свежей петрушкой и дольками лимона.
example2: The chef garnished the soup with croutons and green onions.

en_definition: a set of instructions showing how to prepare a particular dish
example1: I found a recipe for chocolate cake on the internet.
example1_ru: Я нашёл в интернете рецепт шоколадного торта.
example2: My grandmother's recipe for soup is very popular in our family.

en_definition: one of the things you use to make or cook something
example1: The main ingredients for pasta are flour, eggs, and water.
example1_ru: Основные ингредиенты для пасты — это мука, яйца и вода.
example2: This recipe needs fresh ingredients to taste really good.

en_definition: a tool or piece of equipment used for cooking or eating
example1: I need different utensils for cooking pasta and making salad.
example1_ru: Мне нужны разные кухонные принадлежности для варки пасты и приготовления салата.
example2: Kitchen utensils include knives, spoons, and forks.

en_definition: the amount of food that is served to one person
example1: The portion size in this restaurant is too large for me.
example1_ru: Размер порции в этом ресторане слишком большой для меня.
example2: I ate a small portion of rice and vegetables for lunch.

en_definition: the style of cooking and food that comes from a particular place
example1: I love Italian cuisine because pasta is my favorite food.
example1_ru: Я люблю итальянскую кухню, потому что паста — моё любимое блюдо.
example2: Chinese cuisine has many different regional styles.

en_definition: a person who does not eat meat or fish
example1: My sister is a vegetarian and only eats vegetables and plants.
example1_ru: Моя сестра вегетарианка и ест только овощи и растительную пищу.
example2: Vegetarians need to get protein from beans and nuts.

en_definition: made from or containing milk or milk products
example1: Dairy products like cheese and milk are good for your bones.
example1_ru: Молочные продукты, такие как сыр и молоко, полезны для костей.
example2: I buy dairy products from the supermarket every week.

en_definition: a strong-tasting substance made from dried plants, used to flavor food
example1: Pepper and garlic are my favorite spices for cooking.
example1_ru: Перец и чеснок — мои любимые специи для готовки.
example2: I add spices from India to make curry for dinner.

en_definition: a green plant or part of a plant used to flavor food
example1: I grow basil and parsley herbs in a small pot on my balcony.
example1_ru: Я выращиваю базилик и петрушку в небольшом горшке на балконе.
example2: Fresh herbs make the food taste much better.

en_definition: the particular taste of food or drink
example1: The flavor of this dish is a mix of sweet and salty tastes.
example1_ru: Вкус этого блюда — смесь сладкого и солёного.
example2: This coffee has a strong flavor of chocolate.

en_definition: soft and easy to bite or chew
example1: The meat is tender and easy to cut with a fork.
example1_ru: Мясо мягкое, его легко резать вилкой.
example2: I prefer tender vegetables that do not need much chewing.

en_definition: hard and making a snapping sound when you bite or break it
example1: I like crispy bacon for breakfast more than soft bacon.
example1_ru: На завтрак я больше люблю хрустящий бекон, чем мягкий.
example2: The bread was crispy on the outside and soft inside.

en_definition: having a sharp, unpleasant taste that is not sweet
example1: Some people like bitter coffee without any sugar.
example1_ru: Некоторым людям нравится горький кофе без сахара.
example2: Dark chocolate is more bitter than milk chocolate.

en_definition: having little or no strong taste or flavor
example1: The soup is too bland and needs more salt and pepper.
example1_ru: Суп слишком пресный, нужно добавить соли и перца.
example2: Plain rice can be bland without any spices or sauce.

en_definition: to flow or make something flow from a container
example1: I pour milk into my cereal every morning for breakfast.
example1_ru: Каждое утро я наливаю молоко в хлопья на завтрак.
example2: She poured tea from the kettle into three cups.

en_definition: to press something firmly with your hands
example1: I squeeze lemon juice over my grilled fish.
example1_ru: Я выжимаю лимонный сок на жареную рыбу на гриле.
example2: Squeeze the orange to get fresh juice for the smoothie.

en_definition: to soak meat or vegetables in a liquid to give them flavor
example1: I marinate chicken in lemon and garlic for three hours.
example1_ru: Я мариную курицу в лимоне и чесноке три часа.
example2: The fish needs to marinate overnight for the best taste.

en_definition: to eat dinner or to eat a meal in a restaurant
example1: We dine together as a family every evening.
example1_ru: Мы ужинаем всей семьёй вместе каждый вечер.
example2: They dined at a fancy restaurant for their anniversary.

en_definition: a small dish of food served before the main meal
example1: We ordered soup and shrimp as appetizers before the main course.
example1_ru: На закуску перед основным блюдом мы заказали суп и креветки.
example2: The restaurant served free appetizers while we waited.

en_definition: a sweet dish served after the main meal
example1: For dessert, we had chocolate cake and ice cream.
example1_ru: На десерт у нас был шоколадный торт и мороженое.
example2: The restaurant has delicious desserts on the menu.

en_definition: a drink, such as tea, coffee, juice, or water
example1: We offer many beverages including juice, tea, and water.
example1_ru: Мы предлагаем много напитков, включая сок, чай и воду.
example2: What beverage would you like with your meal?

en_definition: a book that contains recipes and instructions for cooking
example1: My mother uses an old cookbook with traditional family recipes.
example1_ru: Моя мама пользуется старой кулинарной книгой с семейными рецептами.
example2: I bought a cookbook with recipes from different countries.

en_definition: food that remains uneaten after a meal
example1: I put the leftovers in the fridge for tomorrow's lunch.
example1_ru: Я убираю остатки еды в холодильник на завтрашний обед.
example2: We had leftover rice and vegetables for dinner.

en_definition: to arrange plates, cups, and cutlery on a table before eating
example1: I set the table with plates and napkins before dinner.
example1_ru: Я накрываю на стол тарелками и салфетками перед ужином.
example2: Can you set the table for four people?

en_definition: to wash plates and dishes after a meal
example1: I do the dishes every evening after we finish eating.
example1_ru: Я мою посуду каждый вечер после еды.
example2: Please do the dishes while I prepare coffee.

en_definition: food and other things you need to buy for everyday living
example1: I go shopping for groceries every Sunday at the supermarket.
example1_ru: Я хожу за продуктами каждое воскресенье в супермаркет.
example2: The grocery store is closed on holidays.

en_definition: to come to the end of a period of time and no longer be good
example1: This milk expired three days ago and smells bad.
example1_ru: Срок годности этого молока истёк три дня назад, и оно плохо пахнет.
example2: Check the date to make sure the product has not expired.

en_definition: recently made or picked and not stale or old
example1: I buy fresh vegetables from the market twice a week.
example1_ru: Я покупаю свежие овощи на рынке два раза в неделю.
example2: Fresh bread from the bakery tastes much better.

en_definition: to become rotten or bad and no longer be good to eat
example1: Fruit spoils quickly if you do not keep it in the fridge.
example1_ru: Фрукты быстро портятся, если не хранить их в холодильнике.
example2: The milk spoiled because it was left out of the refrigerator.

en_definition: to thaw frozen food by bringing it to room temperature
example1: I defrost chicken in the refrigerator for several hours.
example1_ru: Я размораживаю курицу в холодильнике несколько часов.
example2: You need to defrost the meat before you cook it.

en_definition: to keep food fresh for a long time by processing or treating it
example1: Grandmother preserves tomatoes and cucumbers in jars.
example1_ru: Бабушка консервирует помидоры и огурцы в банках.
example2: Salt is used to preserve fish and meat for months.

en_definition: the process of getting food that your body needs to grow and stay healthy
example1: Good nutrition is important for students studying at university.
example1_ru: Хорошее питание важно для студентов университета.
example2: The nutrition facts are printed on the back of the package.

en_definition: a unit of energy that shows how much energy food gives your body
example1: One apple has about eighty calories of energy.
example1_ru: В одном яблоке около восьмидесяти калорий.
example2: I need to eat enough calories to stay healthy.

en_definition: a substance found in meat, eggs, and beans that helps your body grow
example1: Chicken and fish have a lot of protein for your muscles.
example1_ru: В курице и рыбе много белка для мышц.
example2: Students need protein to have energy for study.

en_definition: a substance found in bread, rice, and sugar that gives energy
example1: Rice and bread are full of carbohydrates for energy.
example1_ru: Рис и хлеб богаты углеводами для энергии.
example2: Athletes eat lots of carbohydrates before a race.

en_definition: a natural substance in food that your body needs to stay healthy
example1: Oranges have lots of vitamin C to help you fight colds.
example1_ru: В апельсинах много витамина С, который помогает бороться с простудой.
example2: I take vitamin pills every morning with my breakfast.

en_definition: a part of plant food that helps your digestive system work well
example1: Vegetables and beans have a lot of fiber for good digestion.
example1_ru: В овощах и бобовых много клетчатки для хорошего пищеварения.
example2: Eating fiber helps your body feel full longer.

en_definition: good for your body and helping you stay strong and well
example1: I eat healthy food like salad and fish for dinner.
example1_ru: Я ем здоровую пищу, например салат и рыбу, на ужин.
example2: Walking every day is a healthy way to exercise.

en_definition: produced naturally without using chemicals or pesticides
example1: We buy organic vegetables at the farmer's market.
example1_ru: Мы покупаем органические овощи на фермерском рынке.
example2: Organic milk comes from cows that eat only natural food.

en_definition: made at home, not bought from a store or restaurant
example1: My mother's homemade pasta sauce is much better than store-bought.
example1_ru: Домашний томатный соус для пасты у моей мамы намного вкуснее магазинного.
example2: We enjoy homemade pizza more than fast-food pizza.

ru_translation: заказать еду на дом, забрать с собой
en_definition: to buy cooked food from a restaurant to eat at home
example1: We take out Chinese food for dinner on Friday nights.
example1_ru: По пятницам вечером мы заказываем китайскую еду на вынос.
example2: Let's take out pizza instead of cooking tonight.

en_definition: to stop doing or having something
example1: I gave up sugar last year to be more healthy.
example1_ru: В прошлом году я отказался от сахара, чтобы быть здоровее.
example2: She gave up eating meat and became a vegetarian.

en_definition: to be very noticeable or different from others
example1: His cooking skills stand out among all the students.
example1_ru: Его кулинарные навыки выделяются среди всех студентов.
example2: The red curtains stand out against the white walls.

ru_translation: справляться, разбираться с, иметь дело с
en_definition: to handle or take action about a problem or situation
example1: I need to deal with the broken dishwasher tomorrow.
example1_ru: Мне нужно разобраться со сломанной посудомоечной машиной завтра.
example2: Parents deal with many problems while raising children.

ru_translation: ухаживать за, присматривать за, заботиться о
en_definition: to take care of someone or something
example1: I look after my younger sister every day after school.
example1_ru: Я присматриваю за младшей сестрой каждый день после школы.
example2: Who looks after the house while your family travels?

en_definition: to be responsible for someone or something and keep them safe
example1: Parents take care of their children with love and attention.
example1_ru: Родители заботятся о своих детях с любовью и вниманием.
example2: I take care of the plants by watering them daily.

en_definition: to have a friendly relationship with someone
example1: My sister and I get along very well now.
example1_ru: Мы с сестрой теперь очень хорошо ладим.
example2: It is important to get along with your roommates.

en_definition: to make a room or place clean and neat by putting things in order
example1: I straighten up my room every Saturday morning.
example1_ru: Я навожу порядок в своей комнате каждую субботу утром.
example2: Please straighten up the living room before guests arrive.

en_definition: to lift or take something from the ground or another place
example1: I pick up my dirty clothes and put them in the laundry basket.
example1_ru: Я собираю свою грязную одежду и кладу её в корзину для белья.
example2: Can you pick up your sister from school today?

en_definition: to move something to its correct place or into storage
example1: Put away your textbooks and prepare for the test.
example1_ru: Уберите учебники и приготовьтесь к контрольной работе.
example2: I put away the groceries in the cupboard.

en_definition: to make something start working by pressing a switch
example1: Turn on the oven before you start cooking dinner.
example1_ru: Включите духовку перед тем, как начать готовить ужин.
example2: I turn on the light as soon as I enter my room.

en_definition: to make something stop working by pressing a switch
example1: Turn off the television before you go to bed.
example1_ru: Выключите телевизор перед сном.
example2: Did you turn off the stove after cooking?

en_definition: to make a place clean and neat by arranging things properly
example1: I tidy up my desk before I start studying every evening.
example1_ru: Я привожу в порядок свой стол каждый вечер перед тем, как заняться учёбой.
example2: Can you tidy up the kitchen before dinner time?
